title: AIATSIS Map
caption: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Australia is made up of many different and distinct groups, each with their own culture, customs, language and laws.
prompt: What does this source suggest about the diversity of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples across Australia?

title: Why Acknowledging Country Matters
caption: Incorporating welcoming and acknowledgement protocols into official meetings recognizes Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples as the Traditional Owners and shows respect.
prompt: Why is acknowledging Country more than just a formal routine?

title: Birds, Stories and Totems
caption: Stories reflect a powerful worldview towards Country, which values and respects relationships between all living things. Many birds are revered as totems.
prompt: What does this source tell us about how stories and totems help pass on culture?

title: Makasar Traders
caption: From at least 1700, fishermen sailed from Makassar to northern Australia (Marege) to trade and work with local Aboriginal people.
prompt: What does this source suggest about connections between northern Australia and people beyond the continent?

title: Trade and Transformation
caption: Trepang were harvested, boiled, and dried in Australia, then traded back to Makassar and eventually to China following monsoon winds.
prompt: What details show that Makasar trade was organised and regular?

title: Long Trade Routes
caption: Ground-edged axe heads have been found over 1000 km from their source quarry site.
prompt: What does this source suggest about the scale and sophistication of trade networks?

title: Torres Strait Islander Flag
caption: Green represents land, black the people, and blue the sea. The star represents peace, navigation, and the five island groups.
prompt: What does the symbolism of the flag suggest about Torres Strait Islander identity and life?

title: Crocodile Mask
caption: Masks made of turtle shell represent ancestors with an associated totem, frequently the crocodile.
prompt: What can this artefact suggest about ceremony, ancestors, and symbolism?

title: Traditional Canoe
caption: A Dauarti canoe from Saibai, 1931. Seafaring and navigation are core to Islander life.
prompt: What does this source suggest about travel, navigation, or daily life in the Torres Strait?
